description: Rewrite developer documentation (getting-started, cli-reference, filters) to be accurate against v0.2.0 codebase, add two Excalidraw diagrams, delete redundant quickstart.md

userStories.title: Delete quickstart.md and remove references
userStories.description: As a documentation maintainer, I want to remove the redundant quickstart so new users aren't confused by two overlapping guides.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quickstart.md deleted
No remaining references to quickstart.md in other docs, README, or any markdown files
getting-started.md is the single entry point for new users
userStories.notes: Simple deletion. Do a grep for 'quickstart' across all .md files and fix any references.

userStories.title: Update getting-started.md prerequisites through boot
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want accurate install and boot instructions so I can get Flowplane running.
acceptanceCriteria: Prerequisites section lists Docker and Rust requirements accurately
Install step uses git clone + cargo install --path . --locked
Boot step uses flowplane init --with-envoy --with-httpbin
Verify step shows flowplane status and curl /health
Port range (10001-10020) verified against codebase and documented correctly
Read .claude/skills/flowplane-ops/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/flowplane-dev/SKILL.md as source of truth before editing
userStories.notes: Update in-place — don't rewrite from scratch. Keep the existing section structure.

userStories.title: Update getting-started.md expose and filter sections
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want working expose and filter examples so I can proxy a service and add rate limiting.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose section shows CLI only — remove any side-by-side CLI/MCP table
Expose example uses correct current syntax from .claude/skills/flowplane-cli/references/commands.md
Filter create example uses correct nested {type, config} JSON structure
Rate limit verification shows the 200/200/200/429/429 pattern
Read .claude/skills/flowplane-cli/references/commands.md as source of truth before editing
userStories.notes: The filter config structure is the most commonly broken thing — verify the nested {type, config} pattern against the skills reference.

userStories.title: Update getting-started.md explore, teardown, and prod sections
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want accurate explore/teardown instructions and a prod-mode appendix.
acceptanceCriteria: Explore section references current MCP tool count (check .claude/skills/flowplane-api/SKILL.md for count)
Tear down instructions use correct commands
Prod mode section uses make up ENVOY=1 HTTPBIN=1 (not raw docker compose)
Prod mode seed flow updated to match v0.2.0 fixes
Dev/prod comparison table is accurate
Network label bug warning removed if fixed or kept if still relevant (check git log)
Next steps section links to cli-reference, filters, mcp docs
userStories.notes: Read .claude/skills/flowplane-ops/SKILL.md for boot/teardown commands. Check Makefile for make up syntax.

userStories.title: Update cli-reference.md stack management and expose sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I want accurate CLI reference for stack management and expose/unexpose commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Global flags section is accurate
Stack management commands (init, down, serve, status, doctor, list) are correct
Expose/unexpose documents error behaviors: 409 for name conflicts, proper 4xx for validation
Input validation rules documented: valid names, positive ports, valid upstream URLs
All examples cross-checked against .claude/skills/flowplane-cli/references/commands.md
flowplane expose --help output included if after_long_help was added (check source)
userStories.notes: Read .claude/skills/flowplane-cli/SKILL.md + references/commands.md as source of truth.

userStories.title: Update cli-reference.md resource CRUD and filter sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I want accurate CLI reference for resource CRUD and filter attach/detach.
acceptanceCriteria: Cluster, listener, route-config CRUD examples match current API
Filter create/attach/detach examples use correct nested {type, config} structure
All JSON payload examples cross-checked against .claude/skills/flowplane-cli/references/commands.md
Import OpenAPI section is accurate
Learning sessions section is accurate

userStories.title: Update cli-reference.md secrets, auth, and config sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I want accurate CLI reference for secrets, auth, and config commands.
acceptanceCriteria: secret create syntax updated — --config flag no longer requires redundant type field
auth whoami documents email/name display in prod mode
Auth commands (login, logout, whoami, token) are accurate for both dev and prod
Config commands (show, path, set) are accurate
Read .claude/skills/flowplane-secrets/SKILL.md for secrets command syntax

userStories.title: Update filters.md filter types and config structure
userStories.description: As a developer adding filters, I want an accurate filter types table and correct config structure documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter count reconciled — check FilterType enum in src/domain/ and compare against skills and existing docs
Filter types table is complete and accurate with all current types
Config structure section documents the nested {type, config} pattern correctly
How filters work section (create -> attach -> verify) is accurate
Read .claude/skills/flowplane-api/SKILL.md + references/filters-quick-ref.md as source of truth
userStories.notes: The filter count discrepancy (skills say 11, docs say 14) must be resolved by checking the actual FilterType enum.

userStories.title: Update filters.md per-filter examples
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working JSON example for each filter type.
acceptanceCriteria: Every filter type has a working JSON config example using nested {type, config} structure
credential_injector example updated to show SDS/secrets integration
Per-route override examples are correct
Detach and delete examples are correct
All examples cross-checked against .claude/skills/flowplane-api/references/filters-quick-ref.md

userStories.title: Create developer workflow Excalidraw diagram
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want a visual overview of the Flowplane development cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Call Excalidraw MCP read_me tool first to learn element format
Create 6-step cycle diagram: build service -> create resources (manual) OR learning session (auto) -> expose -> add filters -> test -> publish
Both paths (manual + learning session) converge at expose step
Dashed green annotation arrow labeled 'This information is already in Flowplane'
Hand-drawn style (roughness: 1), dark red/maroon flow arrows, green annotation arrows
Save to docs/developer-workflow.excalidraw in same JSON format as docs/mcp-tool-call-lifecycle.excalidraw
Add markdown reference to diagram in docs/getting-started.md after Boot the stack section
userStories.notes: Read docs/mcp-tool-call-lifecycle.excalidraw first to match the JSON format. Use Excalidraw MCP tools.

userStories.title: Create architecture overview Excalidraw diagram
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want an architecture diagram showing how Flowplane components connect.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 layers left-to-right: Developer -> Control Plane -> Envoy -> Upstream Services
Developer layer shows 3 surfaces: CLI, MCP tools, REST API/UI
Control plane shows: REST API (8080), MCP Server (/api/v1/mcp), xDS Server (18000 dev / 50051 prod), PostgreSQL
Envoy shows: xDS config reception, proxy to upstreams, ports 10001-10020
Arrow colors: blue (developer), red/orange (xDS config), purple (request traffic)
Component colors: blue (control plane), green (Envoy), gray (upstreams)
Hand-drawn style (roughness: 1), rounded rectangles
Save to docs/architecture.excalidraw
Add markdown reference in docs/getting-started.md Explore section
userStories.notes: Use Excalidraw MCP tools. Match JSON format of existing docs/mcp-tool-call-lifecycle.excalidraw.

userStories.title: Verify all getting-started.md examples against dev stack
userStories.description: As a documentation reader, I need every code example in getting-started.md to work.
acceptanceCriteria: Boot dev stack with flowplane init --with-envoy --with-httpbin
Execute every CLI command shown in getting-started.md sequentially
Verify output matches what the doc shows (or update doc to match)
Execute every curl command shown and verify responses
Fix any failing examples in the doc (not the code)
Report any discrepancies between skills and actual behavior
userStories.notes: This is pure verification — run the commands and fix docs. Do NOT change Flowplane code.

userStories.title: Verify cli-reference.md and filters.md examples against dev stack
userStories.description: As a documentation reader, I need every code example in cli-reference and filters docs to work.
acceptanceCriteria: Dev stack must be running (flowplane init --with-envoy --with-httpbin)
Execute representative examples from each section of cli-reference.md
Execute every filter create/attach example from filters.md
Fix any failing examples in the docs (not the code)
Report any discrepancies between skills and actual behavior
userStories.notes: Reuse the dev stack from US-012. Focus on commands that changed in v0.2.0.

userStories.title: Verify prod-mode examples against prod stack
userStories.description: As a developer moving to production, I need the prod-mode examples to work.
acceptanceCriteria: Boot prod stack with make up ENVOY=1 HTTPBIN=1
Verify prod-specific examples from getting-started.md (seed, auth login, etc.)
Verify auth commands (login, logout, whoami, token) work in prod mode
Verify dev/prod comparison table in getting-started.md is accurate
Fix any failing examples in the docs
Tear down prod stack after verification
userStories.notes: Requires Zitadel running. If prod stack can't boot, document the blocker and skip.
